summary: EC9 Phase 9H checkpoint closure testing (first and only testing_agent pass for the full EC9 checkpoint). Ran a new backend pytest suite (21 tests, /app/backend/tests/test_ec9_phase9h_checkpoint_e2e.py) against the live deployed server covering Pricing Foundation, Grouped Quiz (submit/apply-selected-only/skip), Material Pricing Profiles, Pricing Components, Saved Items + save-as-variation (original preserved), all 6 category calculators (banners, apparel, promotional incl. exact/no-match business-card tiers, vehicle graphics, services, custom unit_price*qty), manual-override preservation, Quote->Order conversion (price identity), recalculate preview/accept/reject (old preserved + new snapshot created), snapshot explain/compare (deterministic, no AI), and Advisory (always unavailable, never mutates price) - ALL 21 PASSED. Also did full Playwright UI end-to-end pass: Pricing Foundation (shop defaults, 9 category cards, Grouped Quiz full flow incl. edit-one-suggested-value + apply-selected-only + Resume-skips-questions + Skip removes draft, CategorySetupWizard opens, Materials/Components/Saved-Items tabs all render correctly with real data), Pricing Calculator (business card exact-tier match $75 for qty 500, no-tier-match manual-required warning for qty 137, Banners with Canonical Material Profile selector changing price $934.76->$1,168.26, Pricing Components selector changing price to $975.30, Apparel/Vehicle-Graphics/Services all render positive prices, Custom unit_price*qty exact at $62.50, manual override shows original suggested price preserved separately). Full Quote lifecycle UI test (create quote -> Detailed line-item entry with embedded calculator -> draft->sent->approved status buttons -> Convert to Order) and Order recalculate-preview Accept/Reject UI both work at the UI-interaction level. However, this deep UI walkthrough surfaced a CRITICAL backend pricing-integrity bug (see below) that the pytest suite's assertions (only checking price>0 and price-identity-after-conversion, not price-correctness-vs-dimensions) did not catch.

critical.issue: For the 4 flat/square-foot categories (banners, rigid_signs, digital_print, cut_vinyl), the shared `_resolve_item_pricing()` helper in both routers/quotes.py and routers/orders.py NEVER forwards the payload's `width_inches`/`height_inches` into `calculate_for_references()`/`calculate_pricing()` (it only passes category, quantity, category_inputs, material_profile_id, pricing_component_ids, saved_item_id). Server-side, `calculate_flat_sqft_pricing` therefore always receives width_inches=None/height_inches=None, and `area_each = max(raw_area=0, category.min_billable_area_sqft default 4.0 sqft)` silently substitutes the category's MINIMUM BILLABLE AREA for the item's real dimensions. Verified via curl RCA: POST /api/pricing/calculate with banners 36x24in qty2 correctly returns selling_price=$350.54 (area_sqft_total=12.0), but adding the IDENTICAL inputs as a Quote line item via POST /api/quotes/{id}/line-items stores unit_price_cents=23369 ($233.69) with pricing_snapshot area_sqft_total=8.0 (=4.0 sqft/unit minimum x 2 units, NOT the real 6 sqft/unit x 2 = 12 sqft). The line item's displayed dimensions label ('36x24in') is correct/stored correctly, but the PRICE is silently wrong - no warning is shown to the user, and the price differs from what the same dialog's own 'Calculate' preview just showed ($350.54 shown in the dialog before Add, but $233.69 actually saved). This affects both the ADD and PATCH/recalculate-preview paths identically, for all 4 flat_sqft categories, on both Quotes and Orders - i.e. the core, most-common sign-shop product pricing is systematically wrong whenever added via the Quote/Order Detailed-entry calculator (the standalone Pricing Calculator page is NOT affected - it calls /pricing/calculate directly with width/height and is correct).
critical.reproduction: 1) POST /api/pricing/calculate {category:banners, width_inches:36, height_inches:24, quantity:2, category_inputs:{}} -> selling_price=350.54. 2) Create a quote, POST /api/quotes/{id}/line-items with the SAME body plus description/selected_price_source=suggested -> returned unit_price_cents=23369 (=$233.69), not the expected ~$175.27/unit (350.54/2). RCA: routers/quotes.py:_resolve_item_pricing and routers/orders.py:_resolve_item_pricing never pass width_inches/height_inches through to calculate_for_references(); services/pricing_flat_sqft.py then falls back to category_defaults.<cat>.min_billable_area_sqft (default 4.0 sqft/unit) as the billable area.
critical.fix_suggestion: Add width_inches/height_inches parameters to _resolve_item_pricing() in both routers/quotes.py and routers/orders.py and forward them into calculate_for_references()/calculate_pricing() (which already accepts these params - services/order_pricing.py:calculate_for_references already has width_inches/height_inches kwargs, they're just never supplied by the two routers). Also update RecalculatePreviewIn (both routers) to optionally accept width_inches/height_inches overrides, and LineItemDialog.jsx's runRecalculatePreview() to pass the currently-edited width/height alongside categoryInputs.

ui_bugs.issue: The CRA webpack-dev-server error overlay repeatedly pops up full-screen with 'ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications' errors (benign, well-known false-positive with radix/shadcn components on resize) during normal interaction (e.g. opening the Grouped Pricing Quiz dialog). It doesn't block force-clicks in automation, but a real user in this preview environment would see a scary red full-screen 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay during completely normal usage. Not a functional bug but noisy UX in this preview deployment.

integration_issues.flow: Quote/Order Detailed line-item entry -> Calculate preview -> Add/Save (flat_sqft categories)
integration_issues.issue: The dialog's 'Calculate' button correctly shows a preview price using the typed width/height (e.g. $350.54/unit), but the price actually persisted after clicking 'Add item'/'Save changes' silently differs (e.g. $233.69/unit) because of the backend bug above - the user has no way to know the saved price doesn't match what they just previewed.

action_items: HIGH PRIORITY FIX: Pass width_inches/height_inches through _resolve_item_pricing() -> calculate_for_references() in routers/quotes.py and routers/orders.py (add/patch/recalculate-preview) so flat_sqft category (banners/rigid_signs/digital_print/cut_vinyl) Quote/Order line items are priced off real dimensions instead of silently falling back to category min_billable_area_sqft.

After the fix, add a regression test comparing a Quote/Order line item's stored unit_price_cents against a direct /pricing/calculate call with identical width/height/category_inputs to prevent this class of bug recurring (my new test_ec9_phase9h_checkpoint_e2e.py did not check this - only price>0 and price-identity-on-conversion, both of which pass trivially even with the wrong area).
Optional cleanup: consider suppressing/tuning the ResizeObserver console error so the CRA dev overlay doesn't full-screen-block the UI in the preview environment (cosmetic only).
critical_code_review_comments: services/order_pricing.py:calculate_for_references already has the width_inches/height_inches kwargs defined and forwards them correctly to calculate_pricing() - the bug is purely in the two routers never supplying them, not in the shared resolver itself. This is a one-line-per-call-site fix in each of the ~5 call sites of _resolve_item_pricing across quotes.py/orders.py.

Everything else reviewed (Pricing Foundation, Grouped Quiz, Materials/Components/Saved Items CRUD, all 6 calculators' dimensionless/non-flat_sqft category paths, tier pricing, manual-override separation, snapshot immutability/lineage, advisory contract) is clean, well-labeled with source_labels/calculation_warnings, and matches the EC09 controlling-document intent described in the module docstrings.

quote_conversion.py correctly copies pricing_snapshot/suggested_price_cents/unit_price_cents byte-for-byte with zero recalculation - verified both via pytest and UI (Q-9 -> O-33 preserved unit price $233.69 exactly, which - while itself derived from the buggy area - correctly demonstrates the NO-RECALCULATION-ON-CONVERT invariant is intact).

success_rate.backend: 100% (21/21 new tests passed; 1 critical business-logic bug found via manual RCA/curl investigation that the automated assertions did not catch)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of requested UI flows executed successfully (all screens rendered, all interactions worked) - 1 backend-caused pricing-accuracy defect surfaced through the UI (see integration_issues)

test_credentials: Used AUTH_DEV_BYPASS dev-login (Dev Shop tenant Owner) per /app/memory/test_credentials.md - no other credentials needed.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_-prefixed customers, quotes (Q-9), orders (O-33), material pricing profile (reused existing 'Test Vinyl Roll' profile if already present), pricing components (TEST Setup Fee x3, TEST Flat Fee For Price Change), saved items (TEST Saved Banner + variations) via both the new pytest file and manual UI/curl RCA testing. Also applied one Grouped Quiz suggestion to the dev-shop tenant's real shop_defaults (production_hourly_rate -> 99.99) during UI quiz testing - a persisted, intentional test of the apply-selected-fields-only feature, not cleaned up (consistent with prior testing-agent sessions' pattern per iteration_18.json context).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The ONE actionable defect is the flat_sqft width/height not being forwarded in Quote/Order pricing resolution (routers/quotes.py and routers/orders.py _resolve_item_pricing helpers) - everything else in EC9 9A-9G is verified working correctly end-to-end on both backend and UI. After main agent fixes this, retest by: (1) POST /api/pricing/calculate with a flat_sqft category+real dimensions, note selling_price; (2) add the identical inputs as a Quote/Order line item via the Detailed dialog or curl; (3) assert the stored unit_price_cents now matches (previously it fell back to category min_billable_area_sqft, e.g. 8.0 sqft total instead of the real 12.0 sqft total for a 36x24in x2 banner). Also re-verify the recalculate-preview path picks up an edited width/height, not just category_inputs.
rca_of_the_issue: routers/quotes.py:_resolve_item_pricing and routers/orders.py:_resolve_item_pricing (both helpers, used by add/patch/recalculate-preview) call `calculate_for_references(settings=..., category=..., quantity=..., category_inputs=..., material_profile_id=..., pricing_component_ids=..., saved_item_id=...)` WITHOUT width_inches/height_inches, even though calculate_for_references() (services/order_pricing.py) already accepts and correctly forwards those two kwargs to calculate_pricing(). services/pricing_flat_sqft.py's calc_banners/calc_rigid_signs/calc_digital_print/calc_cut_vinyl then compute `area_each = max(raw_area(=0 since width/height are None), category.min_billable_area_sqft (default 4.0))`, silently using the category's minimum billable area instead of the real dimensions for every Quote/Order line item priced through the shared calculator path. Confirmed via curl: /pricing/calculate (36x24in x2) returns area_sqft_total=12.0/selling_price=$350.54, while adding the identical payload as a quote line item returns pricing_snapshot.area_sqft_total=8.0 (4.0 min-area x 2 qty) and unit_price_cents=23369 - a different, incorrect price with no warning surfaced to the user.
